name: flask-pluginengine

metadata.name: Flask-PluginEngine
metadata.summary: A simple plugin system for Flask applications.

documentation:
# Flask-PluginEngine

```{image} https://github.com/indico/flask-pluginengine/workflows/Tests/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/indico/flask-pluginengine/actions
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/flask-pluginengine/badge/?version=latest
:target: https://flask-pluginengine.readthedocs.io/
```

A simple plugin system for Flask applications. More detailed documentation available at
<https://flask-pluginengine.readthedocs.io/>.

classes.name: flask_pluginengine.templating.PrefixIgnoringFileSystemLoader
documentation:
FileSystemLoader loader handling plugin prefixes properly

The prefix is preserved in the template name but not when actually
accessing the file system since the files there do not have prefixes.

classes.name: flask_pluginengine.templating.PluginPrefixLoader
documentation: Prefix loader that uses plugin names to select the load path

class_variables.name: flask_pluginengine.templating.PluginContextTemplate.plugin
documentation: overridden on the instance level if a template is in a plugin

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.util.get_state
documentation: Get the application-specific plugine engine data.

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.util.resolve_dependencies
documentation:
Resolve dependencies between plugins and sort them accordingly.

This function guarantees that a plugin is never loaded before any
plugin it depends on. If multiple plugins are ready to be loaded,
the order in which they are loaded is undefined and should not be
relied upon. If you want a certain order, add a (soft) dependency!

```
param plugins

dict mapping plugin names to plugin classes
```

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.util.plugin_context
documentation:
Enter a plugin context if a plugin is provided, otherwise clear it

Useful for code which sometimes needs a plugin context, e.g.
because it may be used in both the core and in a plugin.

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.util.wrap_iterator_in_plugin_context
documentation: Run an iterator inside a plugin context

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.util.wrap_macro_in_plugin_context
documentation: Wrap a macro inside a plugin context

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.util.make_hashable
documentation: Make an object containing dicts and lists hashable.

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.util.memoize
documentation: http://wiki.python.org/moin/PythonDecoratorLibrary#Alternate_memoize_as_nested_functions

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.util.with_plugin_context
documentation:
Decorator to ensure a function is always called in the given plugin context.

```
param plugin

Plugin instance
```

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.util.trim_docstring
documentation:
Trim a docstring based on the algorithm in PEP 257

http://legacy.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0257/#handling-docstring-indentation

classes.name: flask_pluginengine.util.equality_preserving_decorator
documentation: Decorator which is considered equal with the original function

Flask-PluginEngine is free software; you can redistribute it

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.depends
documentation:
Adds dependencies for a plugin.

This decorator adds the given dependencies to the plugin. Multiple
dependencies can be specified using multiple arguments or by using
the decorator multiple times.

```
param plugins

plugin names
```

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.uses
documentation:
Adds soft dependencies for a plugin.

This decorator adds the given soft dependencies to the plugin.
Multiple soft dependencies can be specified using multiple arguments
or by using the decorator multiple times.

Unlike dependencies, the specified plugins will be loaded before the
plugin if possible, but if they are not available, the plugin will be
loaded anyway.

```
param plugins

plugin names
```

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.render_plugin_template
documentation:
Renders a template from the plugin's template folder with the given context.

If the template name contains a plugin name (`pluginname:name`), that
name is used instead of the current plugin's name.

```
param template_name_or_list

the name of the template or an iterable
containing template names (the first
existing template is used)

param context

the variables that should be available in the
context of the template.
```

functions.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.url_for_plugin
documentation: Like url_for but prepending plugin_ to endpoint.

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.Plugin.init
documentation:
Initializes the plugin at application startup.

Should be overridden in your plugin if you need initialization.
Runs inside an application context.

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.Plugin.instance
documentation: The Plugin instance used by the current app

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.Plugin.title
documentation:
The title of the plugin.

Automatically retrieved from the docstring of the plugin class.

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.Plugin.description
documentation:
The description of the plugin.

Automatically retrieved from the docstring of the plugin class.

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.Plugin.plugin_context
documentation: Pushes the plugin on the plugin context stack.

class_variables.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.Plugin.package_name
documentation: set to the containing package when the plugin is loaded

class_variables.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.Plugin.package_version
documentation: set to the version of the containing package when the plugin is loaded

class_variables.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.Plugin.version
documentation: set to the package_version if it's None when the plugin is loaded

class_variables.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.Plugin.name
documentation: set to the entry point name when the plugin is loaded

class_variables.name: flask_pluginengine.plugin.Plugin.root_path
documentation: set to the path of the module containing the class when the plugin is loaded

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.engine.PluginEngine.load_plugins
documentation:
Load all plugins for an application.

```
param app

A Flask application

param skip_failed

If True, initialize plugins even if some
plugins could not be loaded.

return

True if all plugins could have been loaded, False otherwise.
```

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.engine.PluginEngine._import_plugins
documentation:
Import the plugins for an application.

```
param app

A Flask application

return

A dict mapping plugin names to plugin classes
```

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.engine.PluginEngine.get_failed_plugins
documentation:
Return the list of plugins which could not be loaded.

```
param app

A Flask app. Defaults to the current app.
```

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.engine.PluginEngine.get_active_plugins
documentation:
Return the currently active plugins.

```
param app

A Flask app. Defaults to the current app.

return

dict mapping plugin names to plugin instances
```

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.engine.PluginEngine.has_plugin
documentation:
Return if a plugin is loaded in the current app.

```
param name

Plugin name

param app

A Flask app. Defaults to the current app.
```

methods.name: flask_pluginengine.engine.PluginEngine.get_plugin
documentation:
Return a specific plugin of the current app.

```
param name

Plugin name

param app

A Flask app. Defaults to the current app.
```
